evidence.claim: ISO 12233 'Photography — Electronic still picture imaging — Resolution and spatial frequency response' is the recognized international standard for digital camera resolution measurement, distinguishing nominal sensor megapixels from actual usable resolution.
DPReview discussion of megapixel-myth in consumer-camera marketing.

criteria.name: sensor_size
criteria.notes: Larger sensors = more light, better low-light, shallower depth of field.

criteria.name: megapixels
criteria.notes: Weak predictor of quality (E1); sensor size dominates.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Megapixel count above ~24MP shows minimal real-world benefit for most uses; low-light performance and dynamic range depend on sensor size and processing (E1).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the MP claim framed as the primary quality indicator? Sensor size matters more.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Check for crop factor, bit depth (8-bit vs 10-bit), log profiles, recording time limits. Many 'professional' 4K has 30-min clip limits and heavy crops.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Are there crop, bit-depth, or time-limit caveats on the 4K claim?

counterfeitSignals.signal: Gray-market body: international firmware without local warranty

compatibilityQuestions: Do you own lenses for a specific mount? Lens investment typically exceeds body investment; switching ecosystems is expensive.
Primary subject: stills or video? Affects autofocus system and codec priorities.

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Memory cards (fast CFexpress for 4K/8K is expensive)

repairability.notes: Canon, Nikon, Sony have authorized repair programs. Independent specialists can service most issues within 7 years.
